parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: Linda
You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now, answer the questions on your screen. Welcome to Netfone. My name is Linda. Please sit down.

Speaker 2: John
Thanks very much. I've just recently moved into the area and bought a new house, so I need to have a new phone line installed.

Speaker 1: Linda
That's fine. OK, let's first start by taking a few personal details down. What's your first name?

Speaker 2: John
It's John. That's J-O-H-N, not J-O-N.

Speaker 1: Linda
And you said your surname is Thomson? Is that with an H and a P?

Speaker 2: John
Yes, it's with an H and a P. It's spelled T-H-O-M-P-S-O-N.

Speaker 1: Linda
And what is the address of your new house?

Speaker 2: John
It's 14 London Road, Wesley. The postcode is WE62FF.

Speaker 1: Linda
Of course, you don't have a home phone number yet, but can we have a work number or a mobile phone number?

Speaker 2: John
I can give you both. Work is 01836-829-748, and my cell number is 0176-6329-8541.

Speaker 1: Linda
Sorry, could you say the mobile one again?

Speaker 2: John
Of course. It's 0176-6329-8541.

Speaker 1: Linda
That's fine, thank you. Have you had a phone connection with us before?

Speaker 2: John
No. Where I lived before, my connection was through Medicom. Is there any chance of keeping my old number?

Speaker 1: Linda
Where did you live before?

Speaker 2: John
Toronto.

Speaker 1: Linda
That won't be possible then, I'm afraid. With a change in town comes a change in code, and you can't keep the old Toronto code if you're not there.

Speaker 2: John
Ah, yes. I suppose that's logical. Now, the usual way to pay is to set up a direct debit payment, so that what you owe will come directly off your bank account each month. Is that OK with you?

Speaker 2: John
That's fine. Will I still receive a bill through the post, though, so I know how much I've spent each month?

Speaker 1: Linda
Oh, yes. We send out bills each month, unless you'd like to get them by email.

Speaker 2: John
Not right now. With my new job here in Wesley, I will need a new email address, and that's not ready right now. Can I change later on?

Speaker 1: Linda
Yes, you can. You don't even need to come into the office. You can call our helpline for that or do it online on our website.

Speaker 2: John
Good. So, let me take your bank details for your direct debit. I'll need your bank's name, the sort code, and your bank account number.

Speaker 2: John
My bank is London Credit. The sort code is 1340-25, and my account number is 629-861-753.

Speaker 1: Linda
That was a bit fast. Can you say the account number again, please?

Speaker 2: John
It's 629-861-753.

Speaker 1: Linda
Thank you. At the end, I'll print out a confirmation for you to sign.
